mission.title: keda: Support custom formula for multiple metrics
mission.description: Support custom formula for multiple metrics. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current keda deployment
steps.description:
Verify your keda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
helm list -n keda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keda installation.

steps.title: Review keda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get configmap -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keda
```
### Proposal

- KEDA metrics server knows how to get multiple metrics from various sources.
- Currently, the desired size computation works for each scaler individually and the final value is decided according to MAX.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support custom formula for multiple metrics
steps.description:
# UPDATE
Ive separated 2 functions in this PR into 2 and am keeping this as draft only for references as references
- new feat: formula [here](https://github.com/kedacore/keda/pull/4821)
- new feat: grpc servers as external calculations [here](https://github.com/kedacore/keda/pull/4859)
###
```yaml
q := queue-length
cpu := DB-writer CPU utilization
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get events -n keda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support custom formula for multiple metrics" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
# UPDATE
Ive separated 2 functions in this PR into 2 and am keeping this as draft only for references as references
- new feat: formula [here](https://github.com/kedacore/keda/pull/4821)
- new feat: grpc servers as external calculations [here](https://github.com/kedacore/keda/pull/4859)
### Summary
adds new structure in SO that describes how to modify/alter fetched metrics.
codeSnippets:
q := queue-length
cpu := DB-writer CPU utilization

triggers:
  - type: composed
    metadata:
      metrics:
        q_length: 
          type: aws-cloudwatch
          metadata:
            namespace: AWS/SQS
            ... # SQS length
        cpu_util:
          type: aws-cloudwatch
          metadata:
            namespace: AWS/Neptune
            ... # neptune writer CPU
      formula: "ceil((1 - $cpu_util) * $q_length)"
      targetMetricValue: "5"
      minMetricValue: "0"

scaledObject.spec:
  ...
  mutlipleTriggersCalculation:    ### we should find a better name for this probably
      formula: "ceil((1 - $cpu_util) * $q_length)"
      targetMetricValue: "5"
      minMetricValue: "0"
  triggers:
    - type: aws-cloudwatch
       name: q_length     ### name added here
       metadata:
            namespace: AWS/SQS
            ... # SQS length
    - type: aws-cloudwatch
       name: cpu_util       ### name added here
       metadata:
            namespace: AWS/Neptune
            ... # neptune writer CPU

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
